name: Procedural Terrain
description: A rule-driven system that generates playable landforms, surfaces, or height fields from seeds and constraints.
design_notes: Procedural terrain is useful only when generated spaces support the intended play. Terrain generation should be judged by navigation, resource placement, biome readability, performance budgets, and failure recovery, not just visual variety. A validation pass is essential because rare bad terrain can ruin long sessions.
design_purpose: Create large or replayable spaces without hand-authoring every surface.
Support exploration through varied landforms, biomes, resources, and landmarks.
Produce deterministic worlds when seeds, sharing, or debugging require reproducibility.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are exploring a world with scale, variety, and discoverable structure.

parameters.name: chunk_size
parameters.description: Terrain area generated and streamed as one unit.

parameters.name: noise_scale
parameters.description: Sampling scale used by the main terrain noise function.

parameters.name: octave_count
parameters.description: Number of layered noise samples used for terrain detail.

parameters.name: height_quantization_step
parameters.description: Optional step size for terraced or tile-friendly height output.

parameters.name: validation_retries
parameters.description: Attempts to regenerate or repair a chunk that fails playability checks.

edge_cases: Spawn positions can appear inside water, cliffs, caves, or unreachable terrain unless validated.
Chunk boundaries must align height, collision, resources, and nav data without visible seams.
Changing generation rules after release can break existing world seeds and player bases.
Floating-point differences across platforms can desync deterministic terrain if noise functions are not stable.
common_bugs: Visual mesh and collision mesh are generated from different samples and do not line up.
Biome blending creates thin invalid strips that trap players or pathfinding agents.
Generation runs on the render thread and stalls when the player crosses chunk boundaries.
Validation checks only local chunks and misses roads, rivers, or caves that fail across chunk edges.
balancing_notes: Terrain variety should serve navigation and goals; pure noise often creates fatigue rather than exploration.
Resource and enemy placement need to be balanced against terrain difficulty and travel cost.
accessibility_notes: Avoid relying only on subtle color changes to distinguish traversal-safe terrain from hazards.
Provide map, marker, or pathing support if generated terrain can create visually dense navigation problems.

implementation_notes.unity: Generate height, biome, collision, and decoration data from a deterministic chunk seed; use jobs or background tasks for expensive passes where possible.
implementation_notes.godot: Build chunk data in worker threads when safe, then apply TileMap, MeshInstance, or collision updates on the main thread with deterministic seed inputs.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use World Partition, Procedural Content Generation tools, or custom runtime components with explicit seed/version metadata for generated chunks.
implementation_notes.web: Generate chunks as typed arrays or mesh buffers off the main thread with Web Workers when available; keep seed and generator version in save data.

example_games.title: Minecraft
example_games.note: Uses generated terrain and world seeds for large explorable worlds.
